title: Forward-only undercut/overcut economics features from prior pitters
summary: Official F1 strategy analysis repeatedly emphasizes that stop timing depends on track-specific undercut strength, tyre warm-up penalty, degradation, and whether a rival is within striking range.
rationale: Official F1 strategy analysis repeatedly emphasizes that stop timing depends on track-specific undercut strength, tyre warm-up penalty, degradation, and whether a rival is within striking range. Your current features approximate wear windows well, but they do not directly estimate the race-specific value of pitting now versus staying out using only prior in-race evidence.
implementation_hint: For each `(Year,Race,Compound,LapNumber)`, compute forward-only summaries from already-pitted cars only: fresh-tyre out-lap delta, next-lap position gain/loss after pitting, and recent undercut success rate. Interact these with each driver’s own wear state, cooldown state, and local rival proxies (`Position`, neighbor deltas, recent lap loss). Keep all summaries strictly based on earlier laps within race.
expected_effect: Adds a race-specific economics signal that distinguishes tracks or conditions where pitting now is actually rewarded from those where warm-up makes the overcut viable.
risk: Easy to leak if any feature uses same-lap or future post-stop outcomes; sparse prior evidence early in races can make the estimate noisy and should be gated by evidence count.
